ogTitle: Brockley vs Brodies Plains: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Brockley and Brodies Plains.
heading: Brockley vs Brodies Plains — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Brockley, New South Wales and Brodies Plains, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Brockley has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Brodies Plains (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Brockley or Brodies Plains safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Brockley has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Brodies Plains scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Brockley scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Brodies Plains scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Brockley or Brodies Plains?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Brockley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
